examples.title: Defect Density Calculation
examples.description: Calculate defect density to measure code quality
examples.code:
CALCULATE_DEFECT_DENSITY
INPUT: total_defects, lines_of_code
OUTPUT: defect_density

BEGIN
 IF lines_of_code > 0 THEN
 // Convert to KLOC (thousands of lines)
 kloc = lines_of_code / 1000
 defect_density = total_defects / kloc
 
 // Interpret result
 IF defect_density < 1.0 THEN
 PRINT "Excellent quality"
 ELSE IF defect_density < 5.0 THEN
 PRINT "Good quality"
 ELSE IF defect_density < 10 THEN
 PRINT "Average quality"
 ELSE
 PRINT "Poor quality - needs improvement"
 ENDIF
 RETURN defect_density
 ELSE
 RETURN ERROR "Invalid line count"
 ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates defect density (defects per KLOC) which is a standard software quality metric. Lower values indicate higher quality code.

examples.title: Test Coverage Calculator
examples.description: Calculate statement and branch coverage percentages
examples.code:
CALCULATE_TEST_COVERAGE
INPUT: executable_statements, executed_statements
 total_branches, executed_branches
OUTPUT: statement_coverage, branch_coverage

BEGIN
 // Statement Coverage
 IF executable_statements > 0 THEN
 statement_coverage = (executed_statements / executable_statements) * 100
 ELSE
 statement_coverage = 0
 ENDIF
 
 // Branch Coverage 
 IF total_branches > 0 THEN
 branch_coverage = (executed_branches / total_branches) * 100
 ELSE
 branch_coverage = 0
 ENDIF
 
 PRINT "Statement Coverage: " + statement_coverage + "%"
 PRINT "Branch Coverage: " + branch_coverage + "%"
 
 // Quality thresholds
 IF statement_coverage >= 80 THEN
 PRINT "Good test coverage"
 ELSE
 PRINT "Insufficient test coverage - add more tests"
 ENDIF
 
 RETURN statement_coverage, branch_coverage
END
examples.explanation: Calculates test coverage metrics to determine how thoroughly the code has been tested. Industry standard is typically 80% coverage.

examples.title: Quality Assurance Process
examples.description: Main QA activities in a software project
examples.code:
QUALITY_ASSURANCE_PROCESS
INPUT: project_requirements
OUTPUT: quality_report

BEGIN
 // Phase 1: Quality Planning
 quality_plan = CREATE_QUALITY_PLAN()
 standards = DEFINE_STANDARDS()
 metrics_goals = SET_METRICS_GOALS()
 
 // Phase 2: Process Assurance
 PROCESS_AUDIT(quality_plan)
 PROCESS_COMPLIANCE_CHECK(standards)
 
 // Phase 3: Product Assurance 
 reviews = CONDUCT_REVIEWS()
 inspections = CONDUCT_INSPECTIONS()
 static_analysis = PERFORM_STATIC_ANALYSIS()
 
 // Phase 4: Defect Management
 defects = COLLECT_DEFECTS()
 analyzed_defects = ANALYZE_ROOT_CAUSE(defects)
 preventive_actions = DEFINE_PREVENTIVE_ACTIONS(analyzed_defects)
 
 // Phase 5: Reporting
 quality_report = GENERATE_REPORT(reviews, inspections, defects)
 quality_report.risk_assessment = ASSESS_RISK(quality_report)
 quality_report.recommendations = MAKE_RECOMMENDATIONS(quality_report)
 
 RETURN quality_report
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode outlines the QA process from planning through reporting, showing the comprehensive nature of quality assurance activities.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is project size

examples.title: MTBF and MTTR Calculation
examples.code:
CALCULATE_RELATIONSHIP_METRICS
INPUT: total_operational_hours, number_of_failures
 total_repair_time, number_of_repairs
OUTPUT: mtbf, mttr, availability

BEGIN
 IF number_of_failures > 0 THEN
 // Mean Time Between Failures (Reliability)
 mtbf = total_operational_hours / number_of_failures
 PRINT "MTBF: " + mtbf + " hours"
 ELSE
 mtbf = INFINITY
 PRINT "No failures recorded - Perfect reliability"
 ENDIF
 
 IF number_of_repairs > 0 THEN
 // Mean Time To Repair (Maintainability)
 mttr = total_repair_time / number_of_repairs
 PRINT "MTTR: " + mttr + " hours"
 ELSE
 mttr = 0
 PRINT "No repairs needed"
 ENDIF
 
 // System Availability = MTBF / (MTBF + MTTR)
 IF mtbf != INFINITY THEN
 availability = (mtbf / (mtbf + mttr)) * 100
 PRINT "System Availability: " + availability + "%"
 ENDIF
 
 // Interpretation
 IF mtbf > 1000 AND mttr < 1 THEN
 PRINT "High Reliability and Maintainability"
 ELSE
 PRINT "Improvement Needed"
 ENDIF
 
 RETURN mtbf, mttr, availability
END
examples.explanation: Calculates MTBF (reliability) and MTTR (maintainability) metrics along with system availability. These are key indicators of software quality.
